reason: Enabling FIPS mode requires adding the kernel boot argument fips=1 and changing the system-wide cryptographic policy. This change is not supported on an already installed system, can cause boot failures, break cryptographic operations, and potentially lock out administrators. The policy explicitly classifies FIPS changes as too dangerous to remediate automatically.
estimated_impact: reboot required; boot failure; cryptographic incompatibility with existing applications; potential loss of SSH/console access; system instability
